sections.title: Multi-Connection Support
items: Connect to multiple databases simultaneously — each connection maintains its own pool
Tabs are linked to connections: each tab remembers which connection it uses
Switching tabs automatically switches the active connection and reloads the schema tree
Connection color dot shown on each tab to indicate which database it belongs to
New tabs inherit the current active connection
Sidebar shows green dot for all connected databases, not just the active one

sections.title: Saved Queries
items: Loading a saved query opens a new tab (or switches to it if already open)
Duplicate name detection when saving — prompts to overwrite existing query
Tab name syncs with saved query name; custom tab names become the default save name
Saved queries remember the connection they were created with

sections.title: Database Connections
items: Database name dropdown for PostgreSQL and MySQL: fetches available databases when host, port, and user are set
Searchable combobox with type-ahead filtering for database selection

sections.title: UI / UX
items: Splash screen with animated logo, progress bar, and loading status on app startup

sections.title: Database Connections
items: Full native PostgreSQL support: replaced sqlx Any driver with native PgPool, MySqlPool, and SqlitePool for correct handling of all PostgreSQL types (uuid, jsonb, timestamp, numeric, interval, arrays, etc.)
Fixed schema introspection failing on PostgreSQL with 'Any driver does not support the Postgres type PgTypeInfo(Name)' error
Fixed swapped primary key / ordinal position columns in PostgreSQL column listing

sections.title: Editor Tabs
items: Right-click context menu on tabs: Close, Close other tabs, Close tabs to the right, Close all tabs, Rename
Drag a table from the schema tree onto the tab bar to open a new tab with a SELECT statement

sections.title: Query Results
items: Click a row to select it, Ctrl/Cmd+click for multi-select, Shift+click for range select
Selected row count shown in the toolbar
Right-click context menu: Copy (tab-separated), Copy as CSV, Copy as JSON, Copy as Markdown
Keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+C to copy selected rows, Ctrl+A to select all

sections.title: AI Providers
items: OpenRouter model browser: fetches the full list of available models from openrouter.ai and shows a searchable dropdown in the AI provider settings
Disable SSL certificate verification per AI provider — resolves CRYPT_E_NO_REVOCATION_CHECK errors on corporate networks with TLS inspection
Warning shown in the provider form when SSL verification is disabled

sections.title: Database Connections
items: Windows / Integrated authentication for SQL Server connections via the integrated-auth-gssapi feature flag

sections.title: SQL Editor
items: Database-specific syntax highlighting: PostgreSQL (pgsql), MySQL, SQL Server, SQLite
Context-aware SQL autocomplete: suggests tables after FROM/JOIN, columns after SELECT/WHERE, functions, data types, and schema-qualified identifiers
Alias-aware column completion: typing alias. shows columns from the aliased table (e.g. SELECT t. FROM users t)
Dialect-specific SQL functions in autocomplete: PostgreSQL (STRING_AGG, DATE_TRUNC), MySQL (GROUP_CONCAT, IF), SQL Server (GETDATE, DATEADD)
Stored procedures and functions included in autocomplete suggestions
Autocomplete trigger on dot, comma, and opening parenthesis

sections.title: SQL Validation
items: Typo detection with 'did you mean?' suggestions using Levenshtein distance against SQL keyword dictionary
Dialect-specific error checks: LIMIT in SQL Server, TOP in PostgreSQL, ILIKE outside PostgreSQL, RETURNING in MySQL/MSSQL, backtick quoting in PostgreSQL/MSSQL
Trailing comma detection before clause keywords (FROM, WHERE, GROUP, etc.)
DELETE without FROM clause detection
Improved unclosed string/comment error positioning
Validation re-runs automatically when switching database connections

sections.title: Editor Tabs
items: Rename tabs by double-clicking on the tab title
New tab numbering: always increments from highest existing Query number, avoiding duplicates after closing tabs

sections.title: AI Integration
items: Default AI provider selector dropdown in the AI panel for quick switching
Default provider persisted and used automatically on application start
Metadata generation error messages now displayed in the schema tree sidebar

sections.title: Metadata
items: Click green sparkle button on a table to view existing metadata instead of regenerating
Metadata detail modal accessible directly from the schema tree

sections.title: AI-Powered Metadata Generation
items: Generate metadata documentation for individual tables, schemas, or all objects
Non-streaming AI calls for structured JSON metadata responses
Thinking block stripping for reasoning models
Enhanced JSON parsing with fallback extraction from AI responses
Schema-level metadata generation with progress tracking
Metadata UI with tabs and improved spacing

sections.title: Query History & Saved Queries
items: Query execution history with persistence
Saved queries with create, update, and delete support
Query history and saved queries accessible via commands

sections.title: Editor Enhancements
items: Mouse wheel zoom for editor font size (Shift + scroll)
Window state persistence (size, position) across restarts

sections.title: MSSQL Authentication
items: MSSQL authentication method selector: Entra ID, Windows, and SQL Server auth
Azure Entra ID device code flow with user code display and automatic token polling
Trust Server Certificate option for MSSQL connections
Tenant ID and Azure Client ID fields for Entra ID configuration

sections.title: Build & Platform
items: Linux build scripts (bash) replacing PowerShell-only scripts
Build error fix for tiberius Integrated auth on non-Windows platforms

sections.title: AI Integration
items: AI assistant sidebar panel with 4 flows: Generate SQL, Explain Query, Optimize Query, Generate Documentation
7 AI provider types: Claude, OpenAI, Minimax, Z.ai, Claude Code CLI, LM Studio, OpenAI Compatible
Streaming responses via SSE with real-time output in the sidebar
Schema context injection — automatically includes active database schema in AI prompts
Prompt history with persistence (capped at 100 entries)
AI provider settings: add, edit, delete, set default, test connection
Insert AI-generated SQL directly into the editor
Monaco editor context menu: AI Explain Query and AI Optimize Query

sections.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
items: Global keyboard shortcut system with configurable bindings
Default shortcuts: F5 (Run), Ctrl+S (Save), Ctrl+O (Open), Ctrl+Shift+S (Save As), Ctrl+Shift+N (New Connection), Ctrl+Shift+A (Toggle AI), Ctrl+N (New Tab), Ctrl+W (Close Tab), Ctrl+Shift+F (Format)
Shortcuts persisted in localStorage, survive app restarts

sections.title: File Operations
items: Save query to .sql file (Ctrl+S) with native file dialog
Save As (Ctrl+Shift+S) always prompts for file location
Open .sql file (Ctrl+O) into current or new editor tab
Tab title updates to reflect the saved filename

sections.title: Settings Page
items: Settings modal accessible via toolbar gear icon or Ctrl+,
General tab for future editor/appearance/execution preferences
Keyboard Shortcuts tab with full shortcut configuration
About / Releases tab with version history

sections.title: Project Foundation
items: Tauri v2 desktop app with React + TypeScript + Vite frontend
Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui design system with dark/light theme (system preference)
Collapsible sidebar, resizable editor/results split panel layout

sections.title: Database Connections
items: Support for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and SQL Server (MSSQL)
Create, edit, delete, and test database connections
Multiple simultaneous connections with independent pools
Color-coded connection indicators, active state tracking

sections.title: SQL Editor
items: Monaco editor with SQL language mode and custom dark/light themes
SQL keyword autocompletion (90+ keywords, functions, types)
Schema-aware completions: table and column names from connected database
Multi-tab editor with persistence in localStorage
SQL formatting via sql-formatter

sections.title: Query Execution & Results
items: Virtualized data grid (TanStack Table + TanStack Virtual) for large result sets
Sortable columns, row numbers, typed value rendering (NULL, boolean, numbers)
Multiple result sets from semicolon-separated statements
Execution time tracking and error display

sections.title: Schema Browser
items: Tree view: Schema > Tables/Views > Columns with type, PK, nullable info
Double-click or drag-and-drop tables to generate SELECT statements
Right-click context menu: SELECT *, COUNT(*), DESCRIBE, DROP
Auto-refresh on connection change, search/filter
